channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: San Jose employees, meet your new chatbot assistant? City eyes expansion of AI
item.description: San Jose is soliciting proposals for a generative AI platform that can reduce some of the repetitive and administrative aspects of city workers' jobs.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: How to watch Sunderland vs Wolves: live stream Premier League 2025/26 game, TV channels, preview, team news
item.description: All the ways to watch Sunderland vs Wolves as the Black Country club seek their first win of the season on Wearside.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Tesla's self-driving cars under fire again
item.description: Tesla's Full Self-Driving system faces federal investigation following 58 reports of crashes, with six vehicles running red lights before colliding with other cars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new DFARS rule could block defense contracts for firms with foreign consulting ties
item.description: "Even though those carve outs might limit application to specific activities the firms do, it may not completely cover all the activities," said Diz Locaria. The post A new DFARS rule could block defense contracts for firms with foreign consulting ties first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Putting AI agents to work in IT
item.description: Over the next 12 to 24 months, AI agents are expected to revolutionize every facet of business. According to PwC’s AI Agent Survey, 75% of respondents believe AI agents will reshape the workplace even more than the Internet did in the past.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is the 'AI homeless man prank'? Police says it's dangerous
item.description: The prank uses artificial intelligence to create a picture or video of a "homeless man" entering a person's home, going through their fridge, or lying in their bed.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Q&A: IBM’s Mikel Díez on hybridizing quantum and classical computing
item.description: The Basque city of San Sebastián is beginning to play an interesting leading role in the radically different field of quantum technology. The official launch of IBM Quantum System Two, the most advanced quantum computer of the ‘Blue Giant,’ happened in Donostia-San Sebastián.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia’s DGX Spark desktop supercomputer is on sale now, but hard to find
item.description: Nvidia’s “personal AI supercomputer,” the DGX Spark, may run fast but it’s been slow getting here. It finally went on sale on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to London in 2026
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Robotaxi pioneer Waymo plans to expand to London next year, marking the company’s latest step in rolling out its driverless ride service internationally.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: It's called automated officiating. The NBA is utilizing it to get even more calls right
item.description: The play, in real time to the naked eye, might have looked very close to a violation. LeBron James leaped, got his right hand on the ball with a few tenths of the game’s final second remaining and tapped it through the basket to give the Los Angeles Lakers a buzzer-beating win last season.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI–Broadcom alliance signals a shift to open infrastructure for AI
item.description: OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to co-develop and deploy its first in-house AI processors. The move could reshape data center networking dynamics and chip supply strategies as the ChatGPT maker races to secure more computing power for its rapidly growing AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI Codex rivals Claude Code
item.description: Codex CLI is an open-source coding agent from OpenAI, written primarily in Rust, that runs locally on your computer. Codex IDE extension is a coding agent that runs in Visual Studio Code and its forks, such as Cursor and Windsurf.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Know your ops: Why all ops lead back to devops
item.description: In the beginning, there was devops: a new philosophy that aimed to break down the silos of software development and IT operations and combine both teams into a single group with shared responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World
